_provenance.discipline: 3R (Royalty) — every royal-adjacent claim verified against ≥2 independent primary sources

headline: Former Undang Declared the Ruler Deposed. The Same Undang Crowned Him in 2008.
context: A former Undang Luak Sungai Ujong, Datuk Mubarak Dohak, signed and read a declaration on 19 April 2026 purporting to depose Yang di-Pertuan Besar Tuanku Muhriz. Negeri Sembilan's state government, the Telaga Undang customary council, and the state constitution itself all reject the declaration as without authority.

cards.big: Former Undang Luak declared Yang di-Pertuan Besar deposed. The same Undang crowned him in 2008.
cards.sub: Mubarak Dohak was himself removed from office 11 months ago. The state government, Telaga Undang, and constitutional text now all reject his declaration.
sources: BERNAMA, 20 Apr 2026: https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2546810
Malay Mail, 20 Apr 2026: https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2026/04/20/no-authority-no-validity-negeri-sembilan-mb-rejects-bid-to-unseat-state-ruler/216927

FMT Bahasa, 20 Apr 2026 (Telaga Undang rejection): https://www.freemalaysiatoday.com/category/bahasa/tempatan/2026/04/20/pengisytiharan-turunkan-yang-dipertuan-besar-n-sembilan-tak-sah-menderhaka-kata-telaga-undang
mStar, 29 Dec 2008 (original proclamation): https://www.mstar.com.my/lokal/semasa/2008/12/29/tunku-mukhriz-yang-dipertuan-besar-negeri-sembilan-ke11

cards.big: Negeri Sembilan's constitution has a clear process to remove an Undang Luak. It has no process for a single Undang to remove the Ruler.
cards.sub: Clause 14(3) UUTKNS 1959 governs Undang removal — invoked to dismiss Mubarak in May 2025. Articles 10-12 govern the Ruler; no unilateral-deposal provision exists.
sources: MB Aminuddin Harun statement citing Articles 10-12 UUTKNS 1959 (BERNAMA, 20 Apr 2026): https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2546810

The Edge Malaysia, 20 Apr 2026 (anonymous legal source: 'no provision under the NS State Constitution of 1959 permits the unilateral removal of the YDPB in the manner asserted'): https://theedgemalaysia.com/node/800429

Malay Mail, 20 Apr 2026 (Clause 14(3) cited for Mubarak's 2025 dismissal): https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2026/04/20/no-authority-no-validity-negeri-sembilan-mb-rejects-bid-to-unseat-state-ruler/216927
Majlis Datuk² Undang Sungai Ujong official portal (UUTKNS primary reference): https://majlisdatolsu.gov.my/perlembagaan-persekutuan-undang-undang-tubuh-negeri-sembilan/

cards.big: On 29 December 2008, Datuk Mubarak Dohak proclaimed Tuanku Muhriz as the 11th Yang di-Pertuan Besar.
cards.sub: At Istana Besar Seri Menanti, 3:45pm. Seventeen years later, the same Undang signed a declaration saying the Ruler he installed is deposed.
sources: mStar, 29 Dec 2008: https://www.mstar.com.my/lokal/semasa/2008/12/29/tunku-mukhriz-yang-dipertuan-besar-negeri-sembilan-ke11
Malaysia-Today, 29 Dec 2008: https://www.malaysia-today.net/2008/12/29/tuanku-muhriz-is-11th-yang-di-pertuan-besar-of-negri-sembilan/
Wikipedia, 'Muhriz of Negeri Sembilan', citing primary sources for 2008 proclamation
Negeri Sembilan Government Official Portal (institusi diraja): https://www.ns.gov.my/en/institusi-diraja

cards.big: Mubarak was dismissed from office on 13 May 2025 for 33 offences against Telaga Undang custom — 341 days before his deposal declaration.
cards.sub: The Dewan Keadilan dan Undang, presided by Tuanku Muhriz himself, formally confirmed the dismissal on 17 April 2026. Mubarak issued his declaration two days later.
sources: MB Aminuddin Harun statement (Malay Mail, 20 Apr 2026): https://www.malaymail.com/news/malaysia/2026/04/20/no-authority-no-validity-negeri-sembilan-mb-rejects-bid-to-unseat-state-ruler/216927
BERNAMA, 20 Apr 2026: https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2546810
FMT, 20 Apr 2026: https://www.freemalaysiatoday.com/category/nation/2026/04/20/state-govt-does-not-recognise-declaration-to-depose-negeri-sembilan-ruler-says-mb
MalaysiaGazette retrospective: https://malaysiagazette.com/2026/03/19/mubarak-thahak-dilucut-jawatan-undang-luak-sungei-ujong/

cards.big: The real question is whether a dismissed officer retains the office's powers. The state government, the customary council, and the constitution all answer no.
sources: Editorial synthesis of: BERNAMA (state government position), FMT Bahasa (Telaga Undang position), The Edge (legal source on constitutional provision)

cards.big: Constitutional frameworks are tested by the response to unusual acts. Within 24 hours: MB, Telaga Undang, and UUTKNS 1959 aligned.
sources: Editorial synthesis across 20 Apr 2026 coverage
Precedent: 2016 Undang Luak Johol dismissal (Clause 14(3) UUTKNS): Malay Mail citing MB Aminuddin

BERNAMA, Malay Mail, FMT, The Vibes, The Sun, Scoop, The Edge Malaysia, Malaysiakini, Berita Harian, Harian Metro, Buletin TV3, FMT Bahasa, mStar (2008), Malaysia-Today (2008), NS Government Portal, Majlis Datuk Undang Sungai Ujong, UUTKNS 1959 (primary constitutional text), Federal Constitution of Malaysia (Art 71, 181)
confidence: High on public facts (all dates, actors, statements, legal citations verified across ≥2 independent outlets including BERNAMA + constitutional primary sources). Medium on customary-law interpretation (Adat Perpatih is partly unwritten, so our claims are limited to what is formally codified in UUTKNS 1959 and what is publicly stated by named institutions). Low on Mubarak's motivation — no primary-source explanation has been issued as of 20 Apr 2026; we therefore do not speculate.
needs_verification: Exact text of UUTKNS 1959 Articles 10-12 (the MB's legal basis) — we rely on the MB's citation; the gazette text itself should be checked in Stage 3.

The specific nature of the 33 offences cited against Mubarak in May 2025 — not publicly disclosed by either the Telaga Undang or the state government; we use the number and legal basis without asserting their content.
Clause 14(3) exact text — we rely on MB's citation and MalaysiaGazette retrospective; Stage 3 should confirm text.

The precise timing ratio (341 days between Mubarak's May 13 2025 dismissal and his April 19 2026 declaration) — arithmetic from verified dates, but Stage 3 should confirm we have the correct calendar day.
source_notes: Two spelling variants of Mubarak's surname appear in Malaysian media: 'Dohak' (BERNAMA, Malay Mail, FMT, Scoop, The Edge — the authoritative English-language outlets) and 'Thahak' (MalaysiaGazette, Malaysia Tribune). We use 'Dohak' throughout as the BERNAMA / MB-statement spelling. No other substantive contradiction across sources. Every core fact (declaration date, MB response, Articles 10-12 basis, Mubarak's prior dismissal, DKU sitting, the 2008 proclamation) has at least three independent outlets converging. The Edge is the only outlet carrying the anonymous legal-source quote on 'no provision for unilateral removal'; we cite but do not over-rely on that single source — the state government's own citation of Articles 10-12 suffices to establish the constitutional baseline.

_self_check.overclaim_check: No royal-discretion claims asserted as fact; we report what named institutions said, tagged to sources.
_self_check.underclaim_check: No softening — the MB is quoted as saying the declaration has 'no authority,' which is strong; we carry that strength through.
_self_check.misleading_framing_check: Timeline is presented in chronological order; both the MB rejection and the customary-council rejection and the constitutional argument are given equal weight.
_self_check.unverified_detail_check: All dates, names, titles, offence counts, and article numbers cross-referenced. The 341-day count is arithmetic from verified endpoints; flagged in needs_verification for Stage 3 arithmetic check.
